title: DFS
bullets: Introduce Depth-First Search (DFS) as a graph traversal algorithm.
Explain the core concept of exploring as far as possible along each branch before backtracking.
Detail the recursive nature of DFS implementation.
Describe the use of a stack (implicitly or explicitly) to manage the traversal order.
Illustrate how DFS systematically visits all reachable nodes in a graph.
Highlight the efficiency of DFS for certain graph problems, particularly those with limited depth.
Connect DFS to common algorithmic patterns like those found on LeetCode.
